news.title: Karnataka government provides housing to 427 families in Madikeri
news.para: The Karnataka government is providing housing facilities to the poorest citizens. Eligible families in Madikeri taluk are receiving documents for homes under the Basava and Ambedkar Housing Schemes. The district has received funds for house construction. In Virajpet constituency, 527 houses are planned. The first phase will shelter 427 families.

news.title: Arnya Realestates Fund invests over ₹1,000 crore across 11 housing projects
news.para: Arnya’s debt strategy is expected to reach a total deployment commitment of Rs 1,200 crore, including direct investments, by the time of its final close, which is targeted for June end. The firm is also planning to launch the second series of its debt strategy during the second quarter of the current financial year.

news.title: RWA federation seeks fire safety audit of hotels, PGs and highrises in Noida
news.para: Following recent fire incidents, the Federation of Noida Residents Welfare Associations has requested the GB Nagar police commissioner to strengthen fire safety standards. They are demanding inspections of hotels, guest houses, and paying guest accommodations. The federation also seeks verification of Fire No Objection Certificates in highrise buildings and action against illegal constructions.

news.title: Government gives one-time relief for Bengaluru homeowners from obtaining OCs
news.para: Karnataka residents can now get electricity connections for certain residential buildings without occupancy certificates. This applies to buildings up to Ground plus three floors or stilt parking plus 4 floors on plots up to 2,400 sq. ft. The exemption is for buildings completed and applied for connections by May 31, 2026. Electricity companies will conduct site inspections before providing power.

news.title: Tamil Nadu government launches 24x7 online document registration system
news.para: Tamil Nadu has launched an 'Anywhere Registration' system to streamline document registration. Citizens can now register documents online from any location, eliminating the need to visit sub-registrar offices. Approved digitally signed documents will be sent to users' accounts and via WhatsApp.

news.title: Greater Noida authority orders structural audit of Arihant Amber after biker’s death
news.para: A resident died after a concrete chunk fell from an overhead beam in Noida's Arihant Amber society. Following the fatal incident, the Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority has ordered a structural audit of the building. Residents had previously reported repeated issues with peeling plaster and falling debris.

news.title: Finish probe, speed up trial in illegal sand mining case: Madras HC
news.para: Madras High Court has directed authorities to finalize a pending inquiry against officials involved in illegal sand mining. The court also urged CB-CID to expedite the trial. This case concerns the unlawful excavation and transport of sand from the Thamirabarani river to Kerala. An assistant engineer was suspended for allegedly approving farm ponds used for illegal operations.

news.title: Lucknow: Government bans land sale within LDA housing projects on Sultanpur Road
news.para: The Uttar Pradesh government has banned land transactions in the proposed IT City and Wellness City areas of Lucknow. This move aims to stop speculative deals and ensure that only original landowners benefit from the land pooling scheme. The prohibition is effective immediately. Landowners recorded in revenue records will be eligible for developed plots.

news.title: Ghaziabad to start eviction drive in illegal colonies along Hindon
news.para: Ghaziabad authorities are launching a coordinated drive to remove encroachments along the Hindon river, following a state report to the National Green Tribunal highlighting 258 unauthorized colonies discharging untreated sewage. District Magistrate Ravindra Kumar Mandar stressed the need to identify illegal occupations by cross-referencing records and ground conditions, emphasizing zero tolerance for riverbank constructions to prevent floods and ecological damage.

news.title: Telangana government picks Singapore firm for Future City master plan
news.para: Singapore-based DP Architects has been appointed as the consultant to prepare the master plan for Telangana's Bharat Future City (BFC). The firm will develop a comprehensive plan for the 765 sq km area, with an immediate focus on 30,000 acres for land use and infrastructure, including specialized zones for life sciences, EVs, and advanced manufacturing.

news.title: OC exemption for power connection extended to 2,400 sq ft buildings in Bengaluru
news.para: Bengaluru residents can now get electricity connections without Occupancy Certificates. The Karnataka government has extended this exemption to residential buildings on plots up to 2,400 sqft. This move aims to simplify access to essential services. Over four lakh buildings in Bengaluru are expected to benefit. This decision follows a Supreme Court directive.

news.title: Punjab CM launches registration drive for 10 lakh construction workers; waives fee
news.para: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann announced a drive to register and revalidate 10 lakh construction workers for free, aiming to boost welfare scheme coverage. Special camps will be organized in villages and at construction sites to facilitate registrations and skill development, with processing times for benefits significantly reduced.

news.title: Delhi government has zero tolerance for unauthorised construction: CM
news.para: Delhi government is cracking down on illegal construction. Following a fatal fire, authorities inspected 124 sites, issuing numerous notices. A zero-tolerance policy is in effect against unauthorized building and fire safety violations. The government is exploring third-party insurance for buildings, requiring compliance with safety standards. Demolitions and sealing of properties are ongoing across districts.

news.title: MCD demolishes 94 properties, seals 114 in Delhi after hotel fire kills 21
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has demolished 94 properties and sealed 114 others since June 1. This action follows a fatal fire in Malviya Nagar. Enforcement teams are conducting operations across multiple zones. The corporation has intensified demolition and sealing actions against properties violating building bylaws. Many targeted properties were operating without adhering to regulations or safety norms.

news.title: EOW arrests director in ₹100-crore real estate ponzi scam in Uttar Pradesh; five associates still at large
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Police's Economic Offences Wing arrested Mahipal Singh, a director of Karmabhoomi Real Estate Company Limited, in connection with a ₹100 crore fraud. The company allegedly duped hundreds of investors by promising high returns and plot allotments but failed to deliver. Five other directors remain absconding.

news.title: Villages spared as rural registrations see modest hike in Telangana
news.para: Telangana government has significantly increased land rates within Hyderabad's Outer Ring Road, with some areas seeing a 100% hike. Property registration charges in many rural areas and district headquarters have seen moderate increases of 10-20%, with some villages experiencing no revision at all, prioritizing farmers' interests.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh earmarks ₹350 crore for high-rise fire tenders in 11 cities
news.para: Uttar Pradesh's housing department has allocated Rs 350 crore for advanced fire tenders with hydraulic platforms to combat fires in high-rise buildings. This initiative aims to bolster firefighting capabilities in 11 rapidly urbanizing cities, with Ghaziabad set to receive the most advanced equipment capable of reaching 102 meters.

news.title: Delhi HC impleads JK Paper as defendant in the legal dispute over Prithviraj Road bungalow
news.para: JK Paper Ltd is now a defendant in a Delhi High Court case. The dispute concerns a bungalow on Prithviraj Road. Punit Beriwala claims he made an advance payment for the property in 2004. JK Paper acquired the bungalow in December 2021 for Rs 250 crore. Beriwala's counsel informed JK Paper about his client's claim.
